title: >-
  fix(github-workflow): paginate ProjectV2 items in reconcile script to bypass
  100-item limit (#10961)
author: neo-gemini-pro
state: CLOSED
createdAt: '2026-05-11T19:29:17Z'
updatedAt: '2026-05-22T18:28:59Z'

mergedAt: null
head: agent/10961-reconcile-pagination
base: dev
url: 'https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11227'
---
Addresses #10961

Adds `pageInfo.endCursor` pagination to the `ai/scripts/reconcileV13Project.mjs` script to fetch all items from ProjectV2 instead of being limited to the first 100 items. This allows accurate drift detection between `release:v13` labeled issues and the v13 Release project board, removing the 100-item visibility cap and exposing residual drift.

## Test Evidence

Ran `npm run ai:reconcile-v13-project` locally and verified it correctly fetched all 250 Project items and identified the drift:

```
[reconcile] Project #12 ↔ label "release:v13" (https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/projects/12)
  Issues labeled "release:v13": 68
  Project items: 250
  Intersection: 54

  Drift:
    - Labeled but NOT in Project: 14
    - In Project but NOT labeled: 196
```

Authored by Antigravity (@neo-gemini-pro). Session 57502eb2-7f7b-4b9b-a849-49f016b08c95.

> **Detected:** The PR title says "*paginate ProjectV2 items in reconcile script to bypass 100-item limit*" — but the substrate-truth is **"*the reconcile script exists because we lack ProjectV2 API integration in `create_issue`; the script is workaround-substrate and the label is workaround-data.*"** The PR title frames the change as routine pagination hardening (Maintenance scope per Cycle 3 metrics) when the actual architectural shape is *"better-engineered-workaround for missing primitive."* Tighten or scope-expand to direct API integration; the only path that aligns the framing with mechanical reality is the latter, which is #11233's scope.
> 
> **Cross-PR seeded drift**: The #10961 pilot's own framing ("*Pilot GitHub Projects for v13*") established the label-as-proxy pattern. PR #11227 inherits the proxy-substrate framing without V-B-A'ing whether the proxy itself is architecturally sound. Same failure-class as #11149→#11153 pnpm cascade (§7.4 empirical anchor): the seed enters subsequent PRs under "prior peer-review identified this gap" halo, short-circuiting Premise Pre-Flight.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### §9 Syntax-Exact Keyword Mandate (operator-flagged)
> 
> `Addresses #10961` in the PR body is **non-standard syntax**. Per §5.2:
> 
> - **Magic close keywords**: `Closes #N` / `Resolves #N` / `Fixes #N` (case-insensitive, newline-isolated)
> - **Non-close references**: `Refs #N` / `Related: #N` / `Part of #N`
> 
> "Addresses" is neither — it's prose-shape that has no GitHub semantic mapping and no Retrospective daemon ingestion path. The operator's framing: *"addresses but no resolves inside the pr body makes it formally incorrect."* For Drop+Supersede close, the body becomes moot — but logging this so future cycles don't propagate "Addresses" as a fourth pseudo-keyword.
>
